originalTitle: Sixteen Kenyans missing in Russia after army recruitment
title: News About Our Kenyan Friends Who Went Far Away
content:
Hello from Your Kid-Friendly News Reporter!

Hello everyone! It’s me, your friendly news reporter for Africa! I’m here to tell you about some important news, but in a very simple way, just for you. Today, we're talking about some grown-ups from our amazing country, Kenya, who traveled very far away.

Our Friends Traveled to a Faraway Land

Imagine going on a very, very long journey. Some grown-ups from Kenya did just that. They traveled all the way to a country called Russia. Russia is super far from Kenya! It's across big oceans and many, many lands. These grown-ups went there for different reasons. Some went to find work, maybe to help build things or do other important jobs. They wanted to earn money for their families back home. Sometimes, grown-ups go to new places to try new things or to learn. It's a big adventure for them. But just like any trip, it's important to be safe and to let everyone know where you are.

What Does 'Missing' Mean?

Now, here’s the news we are talking about. Some of these grown-ups who went to Russia are 'missing'. What does 'missing' mean? It means we don’t know exactly where they are right now. Their families and friends in Kenya are feeling very worried. It’s like when your little brother or sister goes to play hide-and-seek, and you can’t find them for a little while. But this is a bit different because they are very far away. Their families really want to know if they are okay and where they are. When someone is missing, it means we are looking for them. We want to make sure they are safe and can come back home. Our government and other grown-ups are working hard to find out what happened to them.

Did You Know? Russia is Super Big!

Did you know that Russia is the biggest country in the whole world? It's so big that it crosses two continents, Europe and Asia! It has very cold places with lots of snow, and also beautiful cities. It takes many, many hours to fly from Kenya to Russia. It's a different world there, with different languages and different weather. People travel all the time, but going to a place so far away can be a very big change.

Why Do Grown-ups Travel So Far?

Grown-ups sometimes travel to faraway places for many reasons. Maybe they want to find a special job that helps their family. Or perhaps they want to learn new skills to help build our country better. It’s a way for them to make a better life for themselves and their children. Just like when your parent goes to the big market in town to buy food, grown-ups go to faraway places for important reasons too. It's a big decision to leave home and go to a new country. They miss their families and friends. That's why it's so important that we know where they are and that they are safe.

Staying Safe When You Go Places

This news reminds us all about how important it is to be safe, no matter where you go. Even if you just go to play at your friend's house or walk to the shop, you should always tell a trusted grown-up where you are going. Always stay with people you know and trust. Never go with strangers. These are super important rules for everyone, big and small! Our parents and guardians teach us this to keep us safe. For grown-ups who travel to faraway lands, it’s also important to let their families know they are safe. They can call or send messages. Communication is a big word, but it just means talking to each other!

Who Helps When Someone is Missing?

When people are missing, our government in Kenya works very hard to help. They have special grown-ups who work for our country in other countries. These special grown-ups are called ambassadors. Did you know? An ambassador is like a special friend from Kenya who lives in another country to help Kenyans who are there. They talk to the government of that country to ask for help and information. Our Kenyan government is talking to the grown-ups in Russia to find out what happened to our missing friends. They want to make sure they are safe and get them home if they need help.

Thinking of Our Families

This news makes us think about families and how much they love each other. When someone is missing, their family misses them very much and worries a lot. We send them our good wishes. We hope that the grown-ups who are missing will be found soon and be safe. It's a reminder that we must always look out for each other, even when we are far apart. Let’s all remember to tell our loved ones where we are going and to stay safe. That way, our families won't have to worry, and we can all be happy together.

Your Reporter’s Promise

Your kid-friendly news reporter promises to always bring you important news in a simple way. We care about our community and want everyone to be safe and happy. Thank you for listening!
summary: Some grown-ups from Kenya who traveled to a faraway country called Russia are now missing, and their families are worried, so our government is trying to find them and make sure they are safe.
whyMatters: This is important because when people travel, especially far away, it's very important to stay safe and let your family know where you are, so they don't worry and can help if you need it.

originalTitle: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
title: Let's Talk Nicely: How Leaders Help Countries Be Friends!
content:
Hello, Little Listeners!

Hi there, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to tell you a very important story. Today, we're going to talk about something super special: how big leaders, like presidents, help countries be friends and solve problems. It's like when you and your friends have a little disagreement, but then you talk it out and become even better friends! Sometimes, grown-ups who are leaders need to talk to each other a lot. They talk about things that affect many people, maybe even people in different countries far away. It's their big job to make sure everyone is safe and happy.

What Do Big Leaders Do?

Imagine you are the leader of your classroom. You want everyone to play nicely, share toys, and have a good day, right? Well, a president is like the leader of a whole country! They want all the people in their country to be safe, to have food, and to go to school. But countries don't live all alone. They have neighbors, just like you have neighbors next door. Sometimes, countries need to talk to their neighbors or other countries around the world about big things. This is where presidents become very important helpers. They travel to meet other leaders and talk about how they can all work together. It's a bit like when your teachers meet to talk about how to make school even better for you.

Talking Helps Us Be Friends

When countries have problems, or when they want to make things better, the best thing to do is talk. Talking helps everyone understand each other. It's like when you feel sad or confused, and then you tell your mom or dad, and they help you feel better. Leaders need to listen carefully when other leaders speak. They need to share their own ideas too. It's a big conversation where everyone tries to find the best way forward, so that people in all countries can live in peace. Peace means no fighting, no sadness, just everyone living happily and helping each other. Isn't that a wonderful idea? Did you know? Africa is a very big continent with 54 different countries! Imagine how many leaders need to talk to each other there!

Why Talking Needs Thinking

Now, here's a little secret: sometimes, talking too much or talking without thinking carefully can make things a bit confusing. It's like when you're playing a game, and everyone shouts their ideas at once. It's hard to hear anyone, right? So, big leaders need to be very thoughtful about what they say and when they say it. They need to think about their words before they speak, just like your teachers ask you to think before you answer a question. One wise leader, Mr. Macron, from a country called France, once shared a good idea about this. He said that leaders should be serious and maybe not talk every single day about very big problems. This doesn't mean they shouldn't talk; it means they should talk *smartly*. It's about choosing the right moment and the right words to help everyone understand and agree. It's like when you have a super important idea, you wait for a calm moment to share it, so everyone can listen properly. Did you know? There are almost 200 countries in the whole wide world! That's a lot of leaders needing to talk nicely!

Working Together for Everyone

When leaders talk carefully and listen well, they can find ways for their countries to work together. Maybe one country can help another country build new schools. Or maybe they can help share yummy food. This kind of talking helps build bridges between countries, not walls. It makes the world a friendlier place for everyone, especially for kids like you. When leaders work together, they can solve big problems like making sure everyone has clean water or helping sick people get better. They can make sure that our planet stays healthy too! Did you know? Many leaders from African countries meet regularly to talk about making Africa stronger and better for everyone. They call these meetings 'summits'!

Peace Makes Us Happy

The most important thing about all this talking is peace. When leaders talk carefully and work together, it helps keep peace in the world. Peace means kids can go to school, families can be safe, and everyone can dream big dreams. It means that instead of worrying, we can play, learn, and grow. Peace is like a warm blanket that covers everyone, making them feel safe and loved. So, remember, even though you are small, you can learn from these big leaders. You can learn to talk nicely, listen carefully, and think before you speak. You can learn to be a good friend and help everyone around you. Did you know? The United Nations is a very big group of countries that work together for peace and friendship all over the world!

Being Kind with Our Words

Just like you learn to share your toys and be kind to your friends, leaders learn to share their ideas and be kind with their words. Because when everyone is kind and thoughtful, the whole world becomes a happier place. So, the next time you hear about leaders talking, remember that they are trying to do their best to make the world peaceful and friendly for you and all the other children in Africa and everywhere else. And that's a really important job! Stay curious, stay kind, and keep listening to the news from your friendly reporter!
summary: Leaders like presidents need to talk carefully and thoughtfully to help countries be friends and solve problems, ensuring peace and happiness for everyone, just like kids need to talk nicely to their friends.
whyMatters: This is important because when leaders talk nicely and work together, it helps countries live in peace, meaning kids can go to school, families are safe, and everyone can be happy and healthy without fighting.

content: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.
